Dear Mr. Wallace:
This is a follow-up letter to ny phone calls to you in
regard to my conflict of interest questions. 
I request
written determination from the California Fair PoIiticaI
Practices Commission in regard to tuy situation.
I would like to provide some background to the situation.
In March of 1"990, the City Council of ilorro Bay approved a
conditional use permit for a shopping center located on 30
acres within the City limits and owned by the Williams
Brothers Corporation. The CUP vtas appealed to the Coastal
Commission where it still 
sits 
hopefully the appeal will
be heard at the Conmission's April LggL meeting. Ir{easure H
to reduce the zoning down to 13 gross acres was placed on
the ballot for November 1-990. Both nyself and Ben Luna ran
as candidates for City Council in November l-990. We both
supported Measure H. 
Ben and I and Measure H won the
election.
The hlilliarns Brothers Corp. filed suit against the City of
Morro Bay as weII as Ben and I in February 1991. I have
attached a copy of the lawsuit for your review. The suit
claims that the City acted wrongfully in allowing Measure H
on the ballot and that Ben and I used this issue as a step-
ping stone to our political 
careers.
The question arises now as to whether or not I have a con-
flict 
of interest whenever Measure H or further action on
the CUP is required by the Council. It is my belief that no
conflict exists, however, I would like your ruling on this.

sections.question: The question arises now as to whether or not I have a conflict of interest whenever Measure H or further action on the CUP is required by the Council. It is my belief that no conflict exists, however, I would like your ruling on this.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a City Council member have a conflict of interest in voting on a conditional use permit and related zoning measure (Measure H) when she campaigned in support of the measure and was elected partly based on that position, and is now being sued by the project applicant?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from a city official seeking FPPC advice, not an FPPC response letter. No conclusion or FPPC determination is present in this document. The letter provides detailed background on the situation but is formatted as a request for guidance rather than as a response. Minor OCR errors present in original text.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The question arises now as to whether or not I have a conflict of interest whenever Measure H or further action on the CUP is required by the Council. It is my belief that no conflict exists, however, I would like your ruling on this.

embedding.summary: City Council member Susan Ullen requests a written FPPC determination regarding whether she has a conflict of interest in voting on Measure H and a shopping center conditional use permit, given that she campaigned for the measure, was elected based partly on that support, and is now a defendant in a lawsuit filed by the project applicant challenging the city's actions.
